basis: ADHD symptom and cognitive benefit hold only while medicated; randomized discontinuation produces faster/greater relapse than staying on drug; in the MTA the 14-month medication advantage was no longer apparent by 36 months
caveat: symptomatic + tolerance (stimulant effects wane over years); non-ADHD enhancement is smaller, task-dependent and equally reversible
source: Molina 2009 JAACAP (MTA 36-mo); randomized-discontinuation studies of dexmethylphenidate / methylphenidate-LA / lisdexamfetamine / guanfacine-ER

basis: vigilance and wakefulness effects are state-dependent and reverse on washout
caveat: no persistence claim in the literature

basis: cholinesterase inhibitors are approved for symptomatic management of Alzheimer's; they do not alter disease course and the symptomatic benefit is lost on discontinuation
caveat: speculative preclinical neuroprotection for the class is not clinically established; no durable enhancement in healthy people
source: AD treatment guidelines; cholinesterase-inhibitor deprescribing literature

basis: lecanemab and donanemab slow clinical decline vs placebo in early Alzheimer's - a disease-modifying (trajectory-altering) effect, not reversal or enhancement, demonstrated only in patients
caveat: modest slowing in AD patients; NOT cognitive enhancement and not studied in healthy people; persistence after cessation not established

basis: memantine provides symptomatic benefit in moderate-severe AD; no disease-course modification
